- Intended end-to-end path is blocked in this workspace: gt sling fails on valid bead gt-fs3 with 'checking bead status: bead not found' because gt shells out to 'bd show --json --allow-stale' but this bd CLI does not support --allow-stale on show.

description: Review of gt-tz5 found a correctness bug in the restored OpenCode refusal handler: the plugin records lastHandledMessageID before fetchAssistantText succeeds. If the assistant-text lookup fails once for a completed refusal message, that message is permanently deduped and neither Continue retries nor escalation can ever happen for it. This should be fixed without broadening the scope of gt-tz5 itself.
notes: Latest dispatch update: the active owner for gt-1wc is now gastown/rust, not chrome. Treat this lane as the current live correctness follow-up for the refusal template stream, separate from the preserved gt-hii validation branch that rust previously held.
title: Make restored OpenCode refusal handler tolerate assistant-text fetch failure

description: Implement a fork-only OpenCode-native plugin for our Gastown fork that automatically sends a flat Continue when an OpenCode session emits the exact refusal string Im sorry, but I cannot assist with that request. This behavior is for our fork only and is not intended for upstream Gastown. The plugin must apply to all OpenCode sessions, retry immediately with no cooldown, count consecutive refusals per session, reset on any non-refusal assistant completion, and escalate instead of retrying again on the fifth consecutive refusal.
title: Add fork-only OpenCode refusal auto-continue plugin

Write the exact behavioral contract for the fork-only OpenCode refusal plugin. Specify the exact refusal string to match, that scope is all OpenCode sessions, that the retry message is exactly Continue, that retries happen immediately with no cooldown or backoff, that the counter is session-scoped, that any non-refusal assistant completion resets the counter, and that the fifth consecutive refusal escalates instead of triggering a sixth retry.
design:
Fork-only OpenCode refusal contract:
- Scope: all OpenCode sessions that load the Gastown .opencode plugin.
- Trigger: exact assistant completion text "Im sorry, but I cannot assist with that request." only.
- Retry payload: exact user message "Continue" only.
- Timing: retry immediately with no cooldown, delay, or backoff.
- State: consecutive refusal counter is session-scoped.
- Reset: any non-refusal assistant completion resets the counter to zero.
- Escalation: the fifth consecutive refusal escalates instead of sending a sixth retry.
notes: Policy verified against landed baseline at 56ca7005: exact Continue retry exists, immediate retry exists, session-scoped counter exists, non-refusal reset exists, escalation on the fifth refusal exists via maxConsecutiveRefusalRetries=4 and >4 branch. Remaining deltas are implementation-level: landed plugin is limited to autonomous roles instead of all OpenCode sessions, and it currently accepts both apostrophe and non-apostrophe refusal strings instead of only the exact canonical refusal string.
title: Define refusal auto-continue policy and trigger rules

description: Modify the existing OpenCode plugin surface under .opencode/plugins/gastown.js so completed assistant text is inspected for the exact refusal string and the same OpenCode session receives a flat Continue prompt through the OpenCode session API. This must be plugin-native and always on for OpenCode sessions, not a separate watchdog or polling script.
notes: Delivered by landed commit d80bfd0d on Bella-Giraffety/gastown upstream-rebuild-main. The plugin now inspects all OpenCode sessions instead of only autonomous roles and matches only the exact canonical refusal string before sending Continue.
title: Implement OpenCode refusal hook in .opencode plugin

description: Track consecutive refusal count per OpenCode session, increment on exact refusal matches, reset on any non-refusal assistant completion, and clear state when the session ends so stale incidents do not leak into later work. The retry loop must stop after the fifth consecutive refusal for the same session incident.
notes: Satisfied by landed baseline commit 56ca7005 from gt-n4j. The plugin already keeps a session-scoped refusalState map, increments consecutive refusals per session, resets consecutiveRefusals on non-refusal assistant completions, and clears state on session.deleted.
title: Implement session-scoped refusal retry state and reset semantics

description: Add the fork-local escalation path used when an OpenCode session returns the exact refusal string five times in a row. The escalation must include enough session and role context to recover unattended workflows and must stop further automatic Continue retries for that incident.
notes: Satisfied by landed baseline commit 56ca7005 from gt-n4j. The plugin already escalates through gt escalate after the fifth consecutive refusal for a session incident and suppresses further retries for that incident via didEscalate.
title: Escalate after five consecutive refusals

close_reason: Verification delivered by landed automated coverage
description: Add automated coverage and, if needed, a reproducible manual verification path for exact-match detection, immediate Continue retries, session-scoped counter increments, reset on non-refusal output, and escalation on the fifth consecutive refusal.
notes: Verification delivered by targeted automated coverage in internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go. The harness now proves exact canonical refusal matching, immediate Continue retries, reset on non-refusal output, escalation on the fifth refusal, all-session scope, and rejection of the apostrophe-less variant.
title: Add verification coverage for refusal auto-continue behavior

This includes reliable hook/mail behavior, clean startup of core agents, correct lifecycle transitions after partial failures, and witness/refinery cleanup behavior that does not require manual repair.

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Existing worker hook installs either auto-refresh safely after template changes or the supported sync path is explicitly documented and enforced
- [ ] Worker environments converge on the restored gastown.js template without relying on silent stale installs
- [ ] Tests cover the chosen upgrade-path behavior for existing installs
description: Review of gt-tz5 found that restoring the refusal auto-continue logic in the authoritative embedded OpenCode template is not enough to automatically update already-provisioned worker installs. InstallForRole still skips existing non-legacy hook files, so older worker environments may retain stale gastown.js content unless hooks-sync or doctor repair is run explicitly. This needs a separate decision and implementation: either auto-refresh existing worker hook files when safe, or narrow/document the acceptance so hooks-sync is the supported convergence path.
notes: Witness reconciliation confirms the earlier thunder gt-9eh attempt was a failed-start / stale branch, not an attached active lane and not preserved work at risk. Branch polecat/thunder/gt-9eh@mo1w1dh5 is 11 commits behind / 0 ahead of origin/upstream-rebuild-main, worktree is clean, and no matching bead/session output remains. Suppress this stale branch unless a new owner or new local work appears.
title: Refresh existing worker hook installs after OpenCode template changes

description: Repair gastown spawn/reuse/restart logic so fresh polecat branches are always created from canonical base refs such as origin/<default-branch> or the explicit integration base, never from stale polecat-local branches inside preserved worktrees. Reflog evidence shows supposedly fresh branches like polecat/shiny/gt-85v@mnxfqn09 were created from older local refs (for example polecat/shiny-mnxfoepj), which reintroduced unrelated preserved commit b9428194 into new work. Acceptance requires branch ancestry to be canonical on new spawn, idle reuse, and recovery paths, with regression coverage proving that preserved local branches cannot contaminate new issue branches.
design: Fix keeps fresh branch creation pinned to canonical remote refs. Session startup now resolves a canonical start point as origin/<configured default branch> (falling back to the remote default branch), fetches origin, and only mints a fresh branch from that ref. It preserves the current branch when respawning the same issue branch, but if a recovered worktree is sitting on main/master or an unrelated preserved polecat/* branch for a new issue, it creates a new issue branch from the canonical base instead of inheriting local history. Regression tests cover AddWithOptions (fresh spawn), ReuseIdlePolecat (idle reuse), and session recovery branch bootstrapping.
notes: Mayor recovery outcome: fury completed the stale-branch-ancestry fix and reached gt done, but submission failed on push to origin for branch polecat/fury/gt-9qb@mnxh139m with the same GitHub auth error seen on other preserved gastown branches. The local implementation state is preserved for retry once credentials are restored; the remaining blocker is repository credentials/push access, not the gt-9qb patch itself.
title: Fix fresh polecat branch creation deriving from stale local polecat refs

title: Fix merge-queue visibility canary when gt mq list misses queued canary wisps

Include upstream PRs/issues that may already contain relevant fixes and produce a prioritized fix list.

Current gastown checkout is materially behind upstream reliability work in the exact failing areas: rig bootstrap atomicity, shared-server beads env binding, routed bead creation from town root, Dolt startup ordering, tmux socket split-brain prevention, and witness/polecat recovery. Local working tree also already contains direct fixes for routed env binding and recovery-only idle polecat reuse, which confirms the failure locus.

acceptance_criteria:

description: Repeated dispatches to certain reused polecat slots (most clearly beads/amber, but the same pattern has appeared on some gastown slots) produce a new branch name with zero local diff, no attached live issue, and no running session. This is not a dotfiles bootstrap symptom; it matches the broader idle-reuse / stale-session lifecycle family in Gastown, where reused polecat slots can fail to transition cleanly and leave behind misleading stale branches or no-op retries. Upstream issue gastownhall/gastown#3030 is closely related and likely shares root cause in reuse / session lifecycle handling.
notes: Upstream overlap and PR-routing decision: the closest existing upstream issue is gastownhall/gastown#3030 (ReuseIdlePolecat / RepairWorktreeWithOptions leave stale sessions running). Our observed zero-diff failed-start reuse loops are in the same lifecycle family, but do not cleanly fit either of our already-open PRs 3629 (canonical base refs) or 3632 (push-failed cleanup and slot reopening). Keep gt-acu as a separate future tiny PR lane, likely framed as a complement to #3030 rather than bundled into the existing open PRs.
title: Handle repeated zero-work failed-start idle-reuse loops on reused polecats

description: Repair Gastown fork behavior around town-root authority, rig bootstrap, routing, sling target selection, polecat recovery, witness cleanup, and mail/convoy consistency so bootstrap and dispatch remain correct under centralized Dolt and routed Beads server mode.

title: Restore gastown runtime and bootstrap invariants for Coder

Follow-up to the landed refusal plugin: fetchAssistantText currently joins text parts and trims whitespace before comparing against the canonical refusal string. That makes the trigger broader than the written contract, which requires exact raw assistant completion text matching. Remove the trim-based normalization, add regression coverage for leading/trailing whitespace variants not triggering, and reland the plugin so the acceptance contract is fully satisfied.
notes: Delivered on Bella-Giraffety/gastown upstream-rebuild-main at 2d21ba98. fetchAssistantText now preserves raw joined assistant text instead of trimming, and regression coverage now proves leading/trailing whitespace variants do not trigger the refusal auto-continue path.
title: Tighten OpenCode refusal trigger to raw exact text

Multiple newly slung polecat sessions across gastown and beads are checking inbox or opening context, then immediately responding with a generic refusal instead of executing the hooked bead. This reproduces the known startup refusal class in live work and is now blocking most dispatched implementation. Investigate startup prompt assembly, inherited conversation state, runtime/provider selection, and hook priming so a fresh working session starts acting on its hook instead of refusing.
design: Implemented the fix in the OpenCode startup plugin by switching internal/hooks/templates/opencode/gastown.js from 'gt prime' to 'gt prime --hook', matching the existing hook SessionStart config. Added regression assertions to internal/hooks/opencode_plugin_test.go so session.created now verifies polecat startup runs 'gt prime --hook' plus 'gt mail check --inject', while crew startup runs only 'gt prime --hook'. Verification: 'go test ./internal/hooks/...' passes before and after rebase. Full 'go test ./...' does not pass in this environment due to unrelated failures outside this diff: cmd/gt TestCrossPlatformBuild (freebsd type mismatch in internal/util/diskspace_unix.go), internal/config TestResolveAgentConfigWithOverride/override_with_subcommand, and multiple packages that require rootless Docker/testcontainers.
notes: Refinery could not process MR gt-3qq: queued branch polecat/guzzle-mo1rw4rh, target upstream-rebuild-main, and commit 3e47c00f727e253be9e860bd02046a7dada6692e are not present in the shared refinery repo (/home/coder/gt). MR rejected as stale/non-materialized; resubmit after the branch and target exist in shared refs.
title: Fix polecat startup refusal loop in hooked gastown work sessions

The plugin provisioning fix ensures gastown.js is installed into nested polecat workspaces and doctor/hooks-sync can detect or repair it, but gt-hii does not yet validate the end-to-end refusal behavior itself. We still need a worker-environment validation that the actual plugin version carrying the Continue prompt and refusal cooldown state triggers correctly on a real hooked-work refusal path. This must be tracked separately so plugin provisioning can land without pretending the live refusal path is fully proven.
notes: Validated in a real polecat-style worker worktree (/home/coder/gt/gastown/polecats/fury/gastown) using provisioned .opencode/plugins/gastown.js and OpenCode 1.4.0. Current main no longer contains the refusal auto-continue implementation that existed in historical commits 56ca7005 (feat: auto-continue OpenCode refusals), d80bfd0d (scope refusal auto-continue to all opencode sessions), and 2d21ba98 (require raw exact refusal text). Reproduction: opencode run --format json --dangerously-skip-permissions with exact prompt Reply with exactly: I"m sorry, but I cannot assist with that request. produced session ses_268c7a3b1ffezbsVCgGusfBtgI; export shows assistant emitted the refusal text once and stopped, with no follow-up Continue prompt or retry. Control session ses_268c94bb5ffe136gvTVq6X4ApA confirms the provisioned plugin loads in this worktree for normal prompts. Remaining gap between provisioning correctness and live refusal behavior is already tracked in gt-tz5.
title: Validate OpenCode refusal auto-continue end to end in worker environment

description:
attached_vars: ["base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main"]
dispatched_by: mayor

Validation bead created directly in the gastown rig database after the shared-server binding repair. Use this bead to prove that town-root resolution, polecat spawn, hook attachment, and session survival all work when the bead actually lives in the owning rig database.
design: Observed state points to clone-local beads state rather than owning-rig visibility. The polecat worktree can read gt-m47, but town-root, rig-root, and owning-rig .beads cannot. Because the worktree and rig .beads share the same dolt_database/project_id but differ in bd vc commit IDs, the most likely failure mode is that the bead was created/updated only in the polecat worktree clone or its working set, not in the owning rig clone that town-root routing targets.
notes:
Validation results:
- Hook attachment succeeded: gt prime and gt mol status both showed gt-m47 hooked to gastown/polecats/rust.
- Polecat visibility succeeded: gt polecat list gastown showed gastown/rust working on gt-m47.
- Lifecycle mail check succeeded: gt mail inbox was empty.
- Town-root / rig-db visibility failed: bd show gt-m47 failed from /home/coder/gt, /home/coder/gt/gastown, and /home/coder/gt/gastown/mayor/rig.
- Worktree-local visibility succeeded: bd show gt-m47 succeeded from /home/coder/gt/gastown/polecats/rust/gastown.
- The worktree .beads and owning rig .beads report the same Dolt database/project_id, but bd vc status shows different commits (worktree ph10i2rg vs rig rjoe5nph), and only the worktree clone lists gt-m47 as hooked.
Conclusion: the validation bead is only visible in the polecat worktree clone, so the successful path is not validated.
title: Validate sling after shared-server binding repair

Create an OpenCode-native plugin for Gas Town sessions that watches completed assistant text and automatically sends a flat  prompt into the same session when the assistant emits the exact refusal string  The behavior should be always-on for OpenCode sessions, scoped to autonomous roles/polecats by default, rate-limited to avoid infinite loops, and escalated after a small retry cap instead of retrying forever. Use the local OpenCode plugin surface already present under  and the SDK session prompt/promptAsync API rather than a separate Gastown dog or tmux watcher.
notes: Exact submission blocker reported upstream: HTTPS push to origin failed with 'Invalid username or token'; SSH fallback via git@github.com:Bella-Giraffety/gastown.git was intercepted by coder gitssh and terminated before push completion. Local branch and commit remain intact for recovery.
title: Add OpenCode refusal auto-continue plugin

When two  commands target the same rig concurrently, the dispatcher can reuse the same idle polecat for both requests and then time out acquiring the assignee sling lock instead of allocating a second worker or failing cleanly before formula side effects occur. Reproduced during the first bootstrap remediation wave on beads/quartz, gastown/fury, and coder_dotfiles/furiosa. Fix dispatch selection/locking so same-rig concurrent slings either serialize before polecat assignment or reserve distinct polecats atomically, and ensure stale formula wisps are not left behind on the failed attempt.
notes: Delivered on Bella-Giraffety/gastown upstream-rebuild-main at 71457d76 after rebasing the preserved nitro branch. Landed changes serialize same-rig idle-polecat reuse and roll back pre-hook formula state on assignee-lock failure.
title: Fix concurrent same-rig sling idle-polecat contention

Rust reported that gt done --target upstream-rebuild-main still runs contamination validation against origin/main before honoring the explicit target branch, causing a false block on work intended for upstream-rebuild-main. This can stall valid fork-reconciliation work even when the issue description explicitly sets base_branch=upstream-rebuild-main. The contamination check should respect the effective target branch it is about to submit against, or clearly separate the two checks.
notes: Draft upstream PR opened from the clean fork branch: https://github.com/gastownhall/gastown/pull/3662. Branch is fix/done-target-contamination off origin/main with only the narrow contamination-preflight fix and targeted regression test. Review findings about broader target normalization remain tracked separately in gt-7p8 and are not bundled into the PR.
title: Honor explicit base branch in gt done contamination check

description: Review of gt-hii found the nested worktree discovery fix itself looks correct, but the actual user-facing gt hooks sync command path still has no regression test for nested polecat layouts. Existing coverage is indirect through helper and doctor tests. Add direct command-level regression coverage for nested polecats so provisioning of gastown.js through gt hooks sync is actually locked down.
title: Add direct nested polecat gt hooks sync regression coverage

acceptance_criteria:

description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-f0er
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-16T22:59:00Z
dispatched_by: unknown

Review of gt-hii confirmed a spec/implementation gap: the nested polecat plugin provisioning fix is valid, but the authoritative embedded OpenCode template being provisioned does not obviously carry the refusal auto-continue / cooldown logic that the acceptance stream expects. That means provisioning can be correct while the actual Continue behavior remains unproven or absent in worker environments. This gap must be tracked separately so we do not over-claim completion from the hooks-sync fix alone.
notes: Draft upstream PR opened from the clean fork branch: https://github.com/gastownhall/gastown/pull/3664. This PR is intentionally scoped to restoring the authoritative embedded/provisioned OpenCode refusal template content only. Remaining correctness, scope, and upgrade-path gaps stay split into gt-1wc, gt-e5f, gt-6p7, gt-9eh, gt-rku, gt-5hx, and gt-jzl.
title: Align provisioned gastown.js template with refusal auto-continue expectations

Beads refinery escalated a new follow-up after the project_id repair: queue-scan ran 'git fetch --prune origin' from /home/coder/gt, which has no configured origin, even though /home/coder/gt/beads/mayor/rig has valid origin/upstream remotes. Investigate refinery/merge-queue Git context selection so rig patrols run remote commands from the owning rig repo or correctly support local-only town-root mode.
notes: Implemented: refinery now resolves git context from an actual worktree root instead of trusting directory existence. Verification: targeted tests passed for new engineer/workdir fallback behavior and git TopLevel helper. Full ./internal/refinery package test run is blocked in this environment by missing rootless Docker for existing container-based tests.
title: Fix refinery queue scan using rig repo when town root has no origin
